semantic_journey: Arabic al-shays = ferocity of character; mushayasa = hostility, mutual aversion. Greek halosis = capture, conquest. Ferocity/hostility and military conquest are linked but 3+ steps apart: ferocity -> conflict -> battle -> conquest. Too long.
key_insight: Arabic ferocity/hostility (character) and Greek conquest/capture (military event) are 3+ semantic steps apart.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-kathna = object made from fresh reeds or branches. Greek kolokynthinos = made from gourds. Both are plant-material objects but from completely different plants (reeds vs gourds). Shared plant-material category is insufficient.
key_insight: Reed-based objects and gourd-based objects are from unrelated plants with different uses; shared plant-material category is insufficient.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-khashab = thick coarse wood/timber. Greek lekhesi = on beds/couches. Original reasoning: beds are made of wood. This is a logical inference not a semantic derivation. Every culture makes beds from wood; this proves nothing about lexical kinship.
key_insight: Arabic timber and Greek beds share only the incidental material relationship; logical inference is not a lexical semantic path.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-kads = coming from behind, following in succession. Greek Seleukides = descendant of Seleucus (proper dynastic name). Succession and a proper dynastic name share no semantic path. Proper name exclusion applies.
key_insight: Seleucid is a proper dynastic name from the Greek personal name Seleucus; Arabic succession-from-behind does not generate this name.

semantic_journey: Arabic jaafaliq = bishop/chief of Christians (Catholicos). Greek enkephalos = brain (en+kephale = in+head). Bishop-as-head is a social metaphor; brain-in-head is a literal anatomical term. Different semantic operations. No valid path.
key_insight: Arabic church-head (bishop, metaphorical) and Greek brain (literal anatomical) share only the head metaphor, not a lexical path.

semantic_journey: Arabic khazaa = to take away, cut off, separate. Greek skhiza = a piece of wood cut off, splinter, lath. Core: cutting-off action and its result. 1-step action-to-result: cutting-off -> the-cut-off-piece.
key_insight: Arabic cutting-off/separating and Greek splinter/cut-off-piece share the cutting concept; Arabic is the action, Greek is the result.

semantic_journey: Arabic najkha = to make throat noise; throat vocalization. Greek kunanke = tonsillitis/throat disease (dog-strangling = throat-choking). Core: throat affliction/sound. Both center on the throat as locus of unusual sound or sensation. 1-step drift: throat-noise -> throat-disease-location.
key_insight: Arabic throat-noise and Greek throat-disease share the throat as locus of affliction, though confidence is low.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-kathha = gathering. Greek khalkotheke = bronze-case/treasury (khalko=copper + theke=case). Gathering and a specialized bronze storage case require 3+ steps: gathering -> things-gathered -> container-for-gathered -> specifically-bronze-container. Too long.
key_insight: Arabic gathering-action and Greek bronze-storage-case are in different semantic categories requiring 3+ steps.
